title: Signal
description:
One claim, from one source, about one subject. Append-only; never rewritten to agree with a later claim. Conflicting signals coexist by design and the resolved record carries the conflict openly.

This is the info archetype's frozen signal shape (platform ADR-0045), copied from kdc rather than redesigned: gn_info_scout emits exactly these fields, so a project that renames them would fail data CI on the gnome's first live run. `site_id` means the subject-of-record, which in this dataset is one pack model at one capacity.

Note what is NOT here: there is no `conflicts_with` field. Two signals conflict when they share a `site_id` and an `attribute` and disagree on `value`. That is derivable, so it is derived (scripts/gen_pages.py), never hand-maintained.

site_id.description: The resolved pack record this claim belongs to, or null while the pack is an unmatched candidate. NULL IS REQUIRED HERE, not omitted: the archetype's prompt instructs the scout to emit `site_id: null` with a `site_hint` for a new subject. (`site_hint` is the opposite — omitted, never null, when site_id is set.) CI enforces that a non-null site_id names a real record.

site_hint.description: Free-text identification when site_id is null: '<maker as reported> <model> — <capacity as reported>'. Omit this key entirely when site_id is set.

attribute.description: The attribute claimed, from the profile's vocabulary: capacity_l, weight_g, dimensions, materials, made_in, lead_time, price.

value.description: What the source says, as the source says it.

source_url.description: The exact page the claim was read on — not a site root, never a search-result snippet. CI enforces that its host belongs to a registered source.

collected_by.description: Who read it: a gnome receipt stamp (gn_info_scout/1.0.1) or a console session (console:EPIC4-05). A claim's collector is part of its provenance.

confidence.description: high = the maker or acquirer speaking for itself. medium = reputable press or review. low = anything else.

allOf.description: A claim must identify its subject: either a resolved site_id, or a site_hint naming the candidate.
